titlestmt.author: by Diane J. Ducharme and Thanh Tran

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 1, 2020 at 03:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, memoranda, personal papers and legal documents, many dating from the period of George Townshend, 1st Marquis of Townshend's Lord-Lieutenancy in Ireland. The collection also contains documents relating to Charles, 2nd Viscount Townshend.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
userestrict.p: The Townshend Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Townshend Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: Osborn Shelves Townshend. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from folder titles and compiled from individual catalog cards. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the dealer. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during basic processing.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence and other documents by and concerning members of the Townshend family may be found by searching the finding aid for Osborn Manuscript Files. These include letters and political memoranda by Charles, 2nd Viscount Townshend (1674-1738) and Charles, 3rd Viscount Townshend (1700-1764).

bioghist.head: Townshend Family
bioghist.p: The Townshends of Raynham Hall, Norfolk, England are descended from Sir Roger Townshend (d. 1493), who was a Justice of the Court of Common Pleas and served as legal advisor to the Paston family.

Charles, 2nd Viscount Townshend (1674-1738) was a British Whig stateman who was one of the negotitators of the Treaty of Utrecht (1713) and served as Secretary of State for the northern department under George I.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. 1969 Acquisition, 1648-1805; II. 1973 Acquisition, 1722-1814; III. 1975 Acquisition, 1709-1857; IV. 1979 Acquisition, 1692-1748; V.1980 Acquisition, 1710-1838.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters, commentaries, treaties, memorials, et al. in English, French, Italian, and Latin. Many are copies; several appear to use a numerical code.

scopecontent.p: 9 miscellaneous items found among the papers of George Townshend, First Marquis
